preview_text:
The model favors South Korea at 62%, more than four times the probability assigned to a South Africa victory at 13%. The underdog's path to three points is narrow on the model's estimate, though a draw remains a meaningful outcome at 25%.

The model's spread reflects a moderately lopsided contest. South Korea's dominance in win probability outpaces the draw probability substantially, suggesting the model expects the favorite to control possession and chances. However, a quarter probability on the draw indicates the match is not a foregone conclusion—South Africa retains realistic chances to avoid defeat.

The market consensus diverges notably from the model. Sportsbooks price South Africa's win probability at 25%, a gap of 12 percentage points above the model, and set South Korea at 47%, roughly 15 points lower. Polymarket moves in the same direction, pricing South Korea at 49%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better reflects the true outcome distribution; the disparity may reflect information asymmetries or different methodologies in assessing the teams' relative strength.
